title: Allow Subitem Timeline Summary as an option for Item Dependencies
excerpt: I have created a Summary column that shows the full timeline of an item based on its subtask dates. However, when I go to set the dependencies of my parent items, that Subitems Timeline Summary column is not an option, s…

posts.author: RebeccaS (Rebecca Schelkun)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have created a Summary column that shows the full timeline of an item based on its subtask dates. However, when I go to set the dependencies of my parent items, that Subitems Timeline Summary column is not an option, so I am unable to make my item dependencies work correctly.

Since it’s also not possible to make subtasks dependent on a parent task, it seems impossible to create a timeline and gantt that can be automated by updating the first item in a sequence. All items must be individually updated. This is very time-consuming.

Does anyone have any ideas for how to address this?

Screenshot 2024-05-21 at 11.43.40 AM3372×626 192 KB

Screenshot 2024-05-21 at 11.39.32 AM1402×1546 221 KB

Screenshot 2024-05-21 at 11.43.25 AM2542×468 65.1 KB

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: That is because the parent item column created when you select “Show summary in parent” is a mirror column that might look like a timeline but still is a mirror. The app Rollup Subitems bypasses this by writing the subitem’s timeline summary into a real parent item timeline column.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @RebeccaS

We developed an app called subitem automations that provides robust automation capabilities to subitems. Here’s an example of how it can be used to set dependencies between subitems and their parent item.

Adjusting subitems based on changes in their parent item →

Screenshot 2024-06-22 at 20.10.281268×156 10.5 KB

Rolling upsubitems to their parent item →

Screenshot 2024-06-22 at 20.11.001355×276 13.5 KB

There a bunch more options, some of them are more robust for advanced project management - Like ensuring subitems don’t start after/before their parent item or the other way around etc.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!
